name: WebBL SQS connection pool shutdown investigation
description: Investigate MessagingException while receiving from SQS in WebBL ECS task and identify root cause/fix.

entries.summary: Started ECS/WebBL incident investigation with live AWS context
entries.detail: Validated active profile 642960533737_INTTRA2-QATeam and fetched ECS service/task metadata for WebBL-prod in cluster ANEPRWEBSVC-001. Next step: correlate CloudWatch exceptions with lifecycle and health events.

entries.summary: Collected ECS and CloudWatch evidence for reported webbl exception
entries.detail: Checked cluster ANEPRWEBSVC-001 service WebBL-prod and task streams. Provided stream WebBL-latest-prod/WebBL-prod-Container/7ebc597433714a7fab0c50951bfb1779 contains startup + healthcheck logs only (no MessagingException/pool-shutdown entries). Running task streams 4ad01fb... and 8cdad922... also have no pool-shutdown entries. ECS service events show task replacements due to ALB health check request timeout around 2026-03-31 07:06.

entries.summary: Identified code-level lifecycle risk likely contributing to instability
entries.detail: WebBLApplication.startListener() both registers ListenerManager with Dropwizard lifecycle and invokes listenerManager.start() directly. This can start schedulers/listeners twice. Observed duplicate NetworkDBListener log lines in same task stream at near-identical timestamps, consistent with duplicate scheduleWithFixedDelay registration. This increases load and can contribute to health-check timeouts and unstable runtime behavior.
Investigation performed by GPT-5.3-Codex (model ID: gpt-5.3-codex).

entries.summary: Investigation summary and probable root cause for SQS pool-shutdown error
entries.detail: Reviewed webbl code + ECS/CloudWatch runtime evidence. Reported log stream 7ebc... does not contain the cited exception; it only has startup/healthcheck logs. However, code has lifecycle bug: WebBLApplication registers ListenerManager as Managed and also calls listenerManager.start() directly, causing duplicate listener/scheduler start. Confirmed by duplicate NetworkDBListener log lines in each task stream at near-identical timestamps. ECS service events show periodic task replacement due to ALB health check timeouts. This combination can lead to shutdown/restart races where SDK HTTP pool is closed while SQS receive loop is still active, surfacing as MessagingException with cause "Connection pool shut down".

entries.summary: User provided full stack trace confirming IllegalStateException: Connection pool shut down in running task
entries.detail:
Full stack trace provided by user:
ERROR [2026-03-31 15:40:03.475] SqsMessagingClient: Unexpected SDK error while receiving from queue .../inttra2_pr_sqs_webbl_zip_inbound
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Connection pool shut down
  at PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager.requestConnection
  at ApacheSdkHttpClient.execute
  at ApacheHttpClient.execute
  via MakeHttpRequestStage pipeline

Task: 4ad01fb7680f4b48b43aa8f580c0df1f (currently RUNNING, started 06:10:47)
This means the ApacheHttpClient connection pool was permanently closed while the task was still alive and polling SQS.

entries.summary: Created investigation document with root cause and fixes
entries.detail:
Created comprehensive investigation document at webbl/docs/2026-03-31-sqs-connection-pool-shutdown-investigation.md covering:
- Full exception trace and component chain
- 7 identified issues with severity ratings
- 6 proposed fixes with code samples
- Recommended implementation order
- ECS service timeline
